title: Repair unaligned mmap windows at file boundaries
prompt:
The read-only slice helper in `src/mapped_slice.c` is intended to copy an exact byte range from a regular file through a private mapping. It validates the requested range and aligns the mapping offset to the host page size, but valid reads with an unaligned offset do not describe the complete byte window to `mmap`. Near EOF, a short request can therefore copy beyond the mapping length supplied by the implementation even though every requested byte is in the file. Repair the page/window calculation at its root.

Preserve the public API and status behavior. A nonempty request succeeds only when the entire `[offset, offset + length)` range exists; it must never truncate to EOF. A zero-length request may use a NULL destination and succeeds at, but not beyond, EOF without creating a mapping. Keep arithmetic checked before narrowing or addition, including the file-range subtraction check and the `SIZE_MAX` guard for the page prefix plus requested length. The file must remain opened read-only and mapped `PROT_READ`/`MAP_PRIVATE`. Copy exactly the requested bytes and leave bytes outside the destination slice untouched. All opened descriptors and successful mappings must be released on success and on later failures.

Make the focused correction in `src/mapped_slice.c`. Do not modify the protected header or tests, weaken the strict C17 compiler flags, change the API, or replace `mmap` with another I/O path. Run the provided verification command. It is offline and deterministic; its syscall seam records mapping arguments while providing safe backing for the deliberately undersized baseline window, so it does not rely on a host-specific memory fault.

full_distill.primary_skill: repair overflow-safe page alignment and mapping-window bounds for exact read-only file slices
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the strict C17 build succeeds, and copied bytes happen to be correct under the protected safe-backing seam, but the runtime fixture exits 1 because an EOF-crossing unaligned slice requests 42 mapped bytes where its page-aligned window requires the full 4133-byte span (for a 4096-byte page), and a second unaligned slice similarly omits its three-byte prefix
full_distill.success_contract: every valid nonempty slice maps from the containing page boundary through exactly the final requested byte, copies only the requested range, and unmaps the same window; EOF and zero-length semantics, subtraction-based file bounds, SIZE_MAX overflow rejection, read-only/private access, error statuses, and descriptor/mapping cleanup remain intact
adversarial_factors: the mmap seam records the production offset and length but supplies a safely widened real mapping, making the undersized-window defect deterministic without relying on SIGSEGV, page rounding side effects, or adjacent virtual memory
one request begins five bytes before a page boundary and ends exactly at a non-page-aligned EOF, while another begins three bytes into a later page and ends before EOF
aligned, exact-EOF zero-length, past-EOF, mmap-failure, close-failure, and munmap-failure cases separate the window calculation from established status and cleanup behavior
destination canaries pin the exact requested slice and reject prefix copying or writes beyond the caller's length
protected source-contract checks retain mmap-based I/O, subtraction-first range validation, SIZE_MAX arithmetic protection, and O_RDONLY plus PROT_READ/MAP_PRIVATE access
